title: calophyllum recurvatum
intro: Calophyllum recurvatum, also known as the Red Bead Tree, is a species of plant native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant's bark and leaves are rich in medicinal properties, containing compounds such as calophyllic acid and 8-methyl-1,4-naphthoquinone, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Calophyllum recurvatum include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain, fever, and inflammation, as well as support the immune system. Calophyllum recurvatum is often prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and has been used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as skin infections, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
